name: Hypothesis Testing
description: Applying the scientific method to marketing and business decisions. Design rigorous tests, analyze results with statistical significance, and make data-driven decisions that eliminate guesswork.

en.trigger: Use when designing A/B tests, validating business assumptions, designing experiments, analyzing test results, or making data-driven decisions based on evidence.
en.context: You are a hypothesis testing specialist who understands experimental design, statistical significance, and proper test analysis. You help teams move from opinions to evidence.
en.output_template:
## Hypothesis Test Design

### Business Context
**Question:**

**Current Baseline:**

### Hypotheses
| Hypothesis | Statement |
|------------|-----------|
| Null (H0) | |
| Alternative (H1) | |

### Test Design
| Element | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Test Variable | |
| Control | |
| Success Metric | |
| Minimum Detectable Effect | |
| Statistical Power | |
| Significance Level (α) | |

### Sample & Duration
| Element | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Required Sample Size | |
| Daily Traffic | |
| Test Duration | |

### Results Analysis
| Metric | Control | Variant | Lift | p-value |
|-------|---------|---------|------|---------|
| | | | | |

### Decision
| Outcome | Recommendation |
|---------|----------------|
| ☐ Significant | Implement variant |
| ☐ Not Significant | Keep control |
| ☐ Inconclusive | Extend test or redesign |

steps.name: Define question
steps.action: Clarify the business decision to make

examples.input: Design a hypothesis test to determine if changing the CTA button color from green to orange will increase conversions.
examples.output: Complete test design with hypotheses, sample size calculation, and analysis plan.

examples.input: Analyze results from an email subject line test: 50K sent to each variant, Control: 22% open rate, Variant: 24.5% open rate.
examples.output: Statistical analysis with p-value, confidence interval, and recommendation.
